title: Sovereign Enterprise RSI Operating System
canonicalStatement: GoalOS-native alpha-AGI Ascension uses AGIALPHA to coordinate a large multi-agent sovereign enterprise system that turns verified work into capability, capability into governed operating capacity, and operating capacity into higher-value proof missions over time.

proofCard.title: Sovereign Enterprise RSI Operating System
proofCard.problem: Even a treasury that reinvests verified value is not enough. A sovereign enterprise needs an operating system that turns many proof cycles into coordinated departments, better missions, higher trust, stronger capability, and measurable productive capacity across time.
proofCard.mission: Coordinate a large multi-agent operating theater that converts verified proof work into reusable enterprise capability, then routes that capability into higher-value missions across procurement, support, compliance, research operations, security, and market expansion.
proofCard.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates enterprise-scale proof settlement: sponsor funding, domain mission budgets, builder claim bonds, proof bonds, reviewer bonds, proof-card registration, credential issuance, reputation-linked access, AlphaWorkUnit metrology, reward vault allocations, TreasuryRouter records, CommercializationPerformanceVault milestones, and Chronicle memory.
proofCard.rsi: Sovereign RSI becomes an operating system when the Selection Gate grants scoped upgrade rights to proven artifacts, then uses those artifacts to improve future planning, routing, evaluation, procurement responses, proof missions, budget allocation, rollback paths, and market-facing trust rooms. The system learns efficiently because unproven artifacts cannot shape the next generation of work.
proofCard.value: Creates the strongest current proof-card layer: verified enterprise work becomes reusable capability; reusable capability increases mission quality; mission quality improves trust, revenue potential, and productive capacity; productive capacity funds stronger future agents, tools, reviews, audits, and infrastructure.
proofCard.claim: Experimental proof-card template. It does not claim achieved AGI, superintelligence, investment return, guaranteed ROI, legal/tax/security approval, Ethereum mainnet deployment, or Kardashev Type II achievement. It defines a bounded mechanism for testing whether verified enterprise work can compound into durable capability and productive capacity.

roles: Defines sovereign enterprise goals and risk boundaries
Turns goals into mission portfolios and proof paths
Route procurement, support, compliance, security, and research work
Produce improved workflows, artifacts, and operating procedures
Package traces, before/after evidence, and reproducibility notes
Validate evidence under rubric and challenge window
Test claim boundaries, failure modes, rollback, and unsafe propagation
Allocate verified value into bounded future capability budgets
Grants scoped upgrade rights only to proven artifacts
Publishes public-safe proof cards and private evidence dockets
goals: Convert trust questions into proof missions.
Allow only proven artifacts to shape future work.
Track capability, evidence, and productive capacity across cycles.
Keep private intelligence private while making public proof verifiable.
Move from one-off outputs to proof-backed operating capacity.
plans: Publish and validate Proof Cards 001-006 as public-safe examples.
Run the proof-work loop before any mainnet claims.
Use Proof Cards 002, 003, and 006 in procurement conversations.
Use verified value to fund higher-value proof missions.
Connect every accepted artifact to future routing and audits.
skills: Converts strategic aim into bounded missions
Builds GoalOS jobs with evidence requirements
Uses AGIALPHA for bonds, rewards, proof-card actions, credentials, and settlement
Packages private and public proof surfaces
Decides if an artifact earns upgrade rights
Keeps recursive improvement safe and reversible
Reinvests verified value into better future missions
Turns accepted proof into durable institutional memory
